steps.title: Solve the unknown directly
steps.content: For infinitely many solutions, the left and right sides must be the same linear expression for every $x$. That means both the $x$-coefficients and the constant terms must match.

steps.title: Verify both identity conditions
steps.content: Algebra gives the same result. Match $x$-coefficients: $$2k+4=k+32$$ $$k=28$$ Match constants: $$3k-14=k+42$$ $$2k=56$$ $$k=28$$ Both parts agree, so the equation becomes identical on both sides only when $k=28$.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Substitute $k=28$: the left side is $(60)x+(70)$ and the right side is also $(60)x+(70)$. Therefore the equation has infinitely many solutions, and the answer is 28.
